title: Ball hydraulic clutch
abstract: The present invention relates to a ball hydraulic clutch, which is located between the engine and the gearbox and is designed to operate with any type of gearbox. According to the present invention, the ball hydraulic clutch consists of a metal disc (1) engaged with an engine flywheel (2), a PTO shaft (3) engaged with the metal disc (1) and attached to the rotor (8) of a vane-type hydraulic pump (4) and it also comprises a pressure chamber (7) mounted on the PTO shaft (3). Inside the hydraulic pump (4) is a ball drum (9) attached to the pump rotor (8) and engaged, by selective mechanical attachment means, with the pump stator (15), which is attached to the clutch shaft (6).
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A ball hydraulic clutch comprising a metal disc (1) engaged with an engine flywheel (2), a PTO shaft (3) selectively engaged with the metal disc (1) and attached to a pump rotor (8) of a vane-type hydraulic pump (4) defining a pressure chamber (7) connected to the PTO shaft (3); a ball drum (9) attached to the pump rotor (8) and engaged, by selective mechanical attachment means, with a pump stator (15); a low-pressure chamber (33) and a high- pressure chamber (28) being associated with a clutch shaft (6); the high-pressure chamber (28) being connected to a pressure controller (38) and, through a solenoid valve (37), to the pressure chamber (7) on the PTO shaft (3); the pressure controller (38) being connected to an oil cooling radiator (40) in fluid communication with the low-pressure chamber (33). 2. A ball hydraulic clutch according to claim 1 , in which the pump rotor (8) is fitted with rectangular radial seats (29) inside which metal blades (14) reciprocate, radial passages (12) being connected to an axial passage (11) and a radial passage (10), both communicating with the pressure chamber (7) on the PTO shaft (3); the axial passage (1 1) communicating with a circumferential passage (17) and with radial passages (18) on the ball drum (9); a plurality of balls (20) adapted to exert pressure on a circumferential passage (21) having stator (15) lugs, the stator (15) including a number of inlets (23) which communicate with cross passages (25), radial passages (26) and axial passages (27) from the clutch shaft (6), said passages being connected to the low-pressure (33) and high-pressure chambers (28), the stator (15) further being fitted with a threaded section on which a threaded securing ring (5) is mounted, said ring (5) regulating the distance between the pump rotor (8) and the stator (15), as well as the force applied to the bearings within the hydraulic pump (4). 3. A ball hydraulic clutch for use with a vehicle transmission, the clutch comprising, in combination: a stator disk and a rotor disk; the stator disk having a series of stator seat depressions; the rotor disk confronting the stator disk and having a series of spaced apart rotor seat depressions which can be aligned in confronting opposition to a corresponding series of spaced 

 apart stator seat depressions; and a series of balls adapted to simultaneously fit into the stator seat depressions and the rotor seat depressions so as to mechanically lock together the stator and rotor and thereby produce identical stator and rotor operating speeds and lossless torque transmission. 4. A ball hydraulic clutch according to claim 3, further including a spacer ring defining apertures into which said balls are loosely arranged in a spaced apart configuration corresponding to the spaced apart stator and rotor seat depressions. 5. A ball hydraulic clutch according to claim 4, further including piston means for urging said balls into said seat depressions. 6. A ball hydraulic clutch according to claim 5, further including resilient biasing means connected to said spacer ring means for urging said balls out of said seat depressions.
